title: Basic Usage
text: skater package provides collection analysis utility functions SNP-based kinship analysis, testing, evaluation R package. Functions package include tools working pedigree data, performing relationship degree inference, assessing classification accuracy, summarizing IBD segment data.

title: Basic Usage
text: Pedigrees define familial relationships hierarchical structure. One formats used PLINK genetic analysis tools .fam file.1 .fam file tabular format one row per individual columns unique IDs mother, father, family unit. package includes read_fam() read files format: Family structures imported “.fam” formated files can translated pedigree structure used kinship2 package.2 “fam” format may include multiple families, fam2ped() function collapse tibble one row per family: example , resulting tibble nested family ID. data column contains individual family information, ped column contains pedigree object family. can unnest particular family: can also look single pedigree: plot pedigree:  plot_pedigree() function skater iterate list pedigree objects, writing multi-page PDF, page containing pedigree family: ped2kinpair() function takes pedigree object produces pairwise list relationships individuals data expected kinship coefficients pair. function can run single family: mapped families pedigree Note maps ped2kinpair() ped objects input tibble, relationships shown -family relationships (zero).

title: Basic Usage
text: skater package includes functions translate kinship coefficients relationship degrees. kinship coefficients come ped2kinpair() kinship estimation software. dibble() function creates degree inference tibble, degrees specified max_degree (default=3), expected kinship coefficient, lower (l) upper (u) inference ranges defined KING paper.3 Degree 0 corresponds self / identity / monozygotic twins, expected kinship coefficient 0.5, inference range >=0.354. Anything beyond maximum degree resolution considered unrelated (degree NA), expected kinship coefficient 0. degree inference max_degree default 3. Change argument allow granular degree inference ranges: Note distance relationship degrees becomes smaller relationship degree becomes distant. dibble() function throw warning max_degree >=10, stop error >=12. kin2degree() function infers relationship degree given kinship coefficient max_degree anything distant treated unrelated. Example first degree relative: Example 4th degree relative, using default max_degree resolution 3: Example 4th degree relative, increasing degree resolution: kin2degree() function vectorized values k, can used inside mutate tibble kinship coefficients:

4      3 0.0625  0.0442 0.0884 # 5     NA 0      -1      0.0442 dibble(max_degree = 5) # # A tibble: 7 × 4 #   degree      k       l      u #    <int>  <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl> # 1      0 0.5     0.354  1      # title: Basic Usage
text: estimated kinship converted degree, may interest compare inferred degree truth. aggregated many relationships inferences, method can help benchmark performance particular kinship analysis method. skater package adapts functionality confusionMatrix package4 confusion_matrix() function. confusion_matrix() function outputs list three objects: tibble calculated accuracy, lower upper bounds, guessing rate p-value accuracy vs. guessing rate. Sensitivity = /(+C) Specificity = D/(B+D) Prevalence = (+C)/(+B+C+D) PPV = (sensitivity * prevalence)/((sensitivity * prevalence) + ((1-specificity) * (1-prevalence))) NPV = (specificity * (1-prevalence))/(((1-sensitivity) * prevalence) + ((specificity) * (1-prevalence))) Detection Rate = /(+B+C+D) Detection Prevalence = (+B)/(+B+C+D) Balanced Accuracy = (sensitivity+specificity)/2 Precision = /(+B) Recall = /(+C) F1 = harmonic mean precision recall False Discovery Rate = 1 - PPV False Omission Rate = 1 - NPV False Positive Rate = 1 - Specificity False Negative Rate = 1 - Sensitivity matrix contingency table object . vector reciprocal RMSE (R-RMSE). R-RMSE calculated sqrt(mean((1/(Target+.5)-1/(Predicted+.5))^2))), superior measure classification accuracy benchmarking relationship degree estimation. Taking reciprocal target predicted degree results larger penalties egregious misclassifications (e.g., classifying first-degree relative pair second degree) misclassifications distant relationships (e.g., misclassifying fourth-degree relative pair fifth-degree). +0.5 adjustment prevents division--zero 0th-degree (identical) relative pair introduced. illustrate usage, first take kinpairs data randomly flip ~20% true relationship degrees. can use purrr::pluck() isolate just contingency table: optionally output tidy (longer=TRUE) format, spread stats class:
code: # Function to randomly flip levels of a factor (at 20%, by default) randomflip <- function(x, p=.2) ifelse(runif(length(x))<p, sample(unique(x)), x)  # Infer degree (truth/target) using kin2degree, then randomly flip 20% of them set.seed(42) kinpairs_inferred <- kinpairs %>%    dplyr::mutate(degree_truth=kin2degree(k, max_degree=3)) %>%    dplyr::mutate(degree_truth=as.character(degree_truth)) %>%   dplyr::mutate(degree_truth=tidyr::replace_na(degree_truth, "unrelated")) %>%    dplyr::mutate(degree_inferred=randomflip(degree_truth)) kinpairs_inferred # title: Basic Usage
text: Tools hap-ibd5 capable inferring shared IBD segments individuals. skater package includes functionality take IBD segments, compute shared genomic centimorgan (cM) length, convert shared cM kinship coefficient. addition inferred segments, functions can estimate “truth” kinship data simulated ped-sim.6 read_ibd() function reads pairwise IBD segment format. Input function can either inferred IBD segments hap-IBD (source="hapibd") simulated segments (source="pedsim"). first example uses data hap-ibd output format: order translate shared genomic cM length kinship coefficient, must load genetic map read_map(). Software IBD segment inference simulation requires genetic map. map loaded kinship estimation one used creating shared IBD segment output. example uses minimal genetic map created min_map7 ships skater: ibd2kin() function takes segments map file outputs tibble one row per pair individuals columns individual 1 ID, individual 2 ID, kinship coefficient pair: noted , IBD segment kinship estimation can performed simulated segments. package includes example IBD data format: Notably, ped-sim differentiates IBD1 IBD2 segments. Given IBD1 IBD2 segments weighted differently kinship calculation, accounted processing. example shared IBD calculated separately IBD1 IBD2 type="IBD1" type="IBD2" respectively. can combine results sum IBD1 IBD2 kinship coefficients get overall kinship coefficient:

text: tibble (present) columns sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV, F1 score, detection rate, detection prevalence, balanced accuracy, FDR, , FPR, FNR.  2 classes, statistics provided class.

title: Calculate various statistics from a confusion matrix — calc_stats
text: Used within confusion_matrix calculate various confusion matrix metrics. called confusion_matrix, want can simply supply table. Suppose 2x2 table notation formulas used : $$Sensitivity = /(+C)$$ $$Specificity = D/(B+D)$$ $$Prevalence = (+C)/(+B+C+D)$$ $$Positive Predictive Value = (sensitivity * prevalence)/((sensitivity*prevalence) + ((1-specificity)*(1-prevalence)))$$ $$Negative Predictive Value = (specificity * (1-prevalence))/(((1-sensitivity)*prevalence) + ((specificity)*(1-prevalence)))$$ $$Detection Rate = /(+B+C+D)$$ $$Detection Prevalence = (+B)/(+B+C+D)$$ $$Balanced Accuracy = (sensitivity+specificity)/2$$ $$Precision = /(+B)$$ $$Recall = /(+C)$$ $$F1 = harmonic mean precision recall = (1+beta^2)*precision*recall/((beta^2 * precision)+recall)$$ beta = 1 function. $$False Discovery Rate = 1 - Positive Predictive Value$$ $$False Omission Rate = 1 - Negative Predictive Value$$ $$False Positive Rate = 1 - Specificity$$ $$False Negative Rate = 1 - Sensitivity$$ $$D' = qnorm(Sensitivity) - qnorm(1 - Specificity)$$ $$AUC ~= pnorm(D'/sqrt(2))$$ See references discussions first five formulas. Abbreviations: Positive Predictive Value: PPV  Negative Predictive Value: NPV  False Discovery Rate: FDR  False Omission Rate:  False Positive Rate: FPR  False Negative Rate: FNR

Sensitivity: True Positive Rate, Recall, Hit Rate, Power  Specificity: True Negative Rate  Positive Predictive Value: Precision  False Negative Rate: Miss Rate, Type II error rate, beta  False Positive Rate: Fallout, Type error rate, alpha  function called confusion_matrix, want, can simply supply table function.

title: Compute kinship coefficient from IBD segments — ibd2kin
text: input data pairwise IBD segments prepared via read_ibd. function internally loop chromosome, use specified genetic map convert shared segments genetic units. , function converts shared length kinship coefficient summing \(0.5*IBD2 + 0.25*IBD1\). Note data read read_ibd source="pedsim" returns list separate tibbles IBD1 IBD2 segments. current implementation function requires running function independently IBD1 IBD2 segments, summarizing (adding) corresponding proportions. See examples.

title: Read genetic map file — read_map
text: genetic map come different sources. One source HapMap map distributed Browning Lab (documentation). map file used, non-sex chromosomes can downloaded concatenated single file follows:   Another source sex-specific map ("bherer") originally published Bherer et al recommended developers ped-sim simulating IBD segments (documentation). retrieve prep map file simulation:   , sexspec.pedsim.map file ready use simulation. However, must averaged reformatted "Plink format" use :   #' genetic maps created tens megabytes size range. trivial store systems reduced version increase portability ease testing. "minimum viable genetic map" used testing installed package data R package example analysis. Read minimum viable genetic maps : Blog post: https://hapi-dna.org/2020/11/minimal-viable-genetic-maps/ Github repo python code: https://github.com/williamslab/min_map code written reduces averaged sex-specific genetic map 833776 28726 positions (~30X reduction!).   averaged version Bherer sex-specific map, reduced minimum viable genetic map 5% error, Plink format, available installed package data (see examples). useful testing code, full genetic map used analysis operations.
code: wget https://bochet.gcc.biostat.washington.edu/beagle/genetic_maps/plink.GRCh37.map.zip unzip plink.GRCh37.map.zip cat *chr[0-9]*GRCh37.map | sort -k1,1 -k4,4 --numeric-sort > plink.allchr.GRCh37.map # Get the refined genetic map and extract wget --no-check-certificate https://github.com/cbherer/Bherer_etal_SexualDimorphismRecombination/raw/master/Refined_genetic_map_b37.tar.gz tar xvfpz Refined_genetic_map_b37.tar.gz  # Format for ped-sim as per https://github.com/williamslab/ped-sim#map-file- printf "#chr\tpos\tmale_cM\tfemale_cM\n" > sexspec.pedsim.map for chr in {1..22}; do   paste Refined_genetic_map_b37/male_chr$chr.txt Refined_genetic_map_b37/female_chr$chr.txt \     | awk -v OFS="\t" 'NR > 1 && $2 == $6 {print $1,$2,$4,$8}' \     | sed 's/^chr//' >> sexspec.pedsim.map; done  # Clean up rm -rf Refined_genetic_map_b37* cat sexspec.pedsim.map | grep -v "^#" | awk -v OFS="\t" '{print $1,".",($3+$4)/2,$2}' > sexspec-avg.plink.map # Get minmap script from github wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/williamslab/min_map/main/min_map.py  # Create empty minmap echo -n > sexspec-avg-min.plink.map  # For each autosome... for chr in {1..22}; do   echo "Working on chromosome $chr..."   # First pull out just one chromosome   grep "^${chr}[[:space:]]" sexspec-avg.plink.map > tmp.${chr}   # Run the python script on that chromosome.   # The genetic map column is 3rd column (2nd in 0-start). Physical position is last column (3 in 0-based)   python3 min_map.py -mapfile tmp.${chr} -chr ${chr} -genetcol 2 -physcol 3 -noheader -error 0.05   # Strip out the header and reformat back to plink format, and append to minmap file   cat min_viable_map${chr}.txt | grep -v "^#" | awk -v OFS="\t" '{print $1,".",$4,$2}' >> sexspec-avg-min.plink.map   # Clean up   rm -f min_viable_map${chr}.txt tmp.${chr} done

what: skater: Utilities for SNP-Based Kinship Analysis — skater-package
title: skater: Utilities for SNP-Based Kinship Analysis — skater-package
text: Utilities single nucleotide polymorphism (SNP) based kinship analysis testing evaluation. 'skater' package contains functions importing, parsing, analyzing pedigree data, performing relationship degree inference, benchmarking relationship degree classification, summarizing identity descent (IBD) segment data. Package functions methods described Turner et al. (2021) "skater: R package SNP-based Kinship Analysis, Testing, Evaluation" doi:10.1101/2021.07.21.453083 .
